author: Joe Raad
description: Toggle, create, rename and reload CSS snippets from a status-bar menu. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Joe Raad
manifest.description: Toggle, create, rename and reload CSS snippets from a status-bar menu.
manifest.name: SnipDock

name: SnipDock

releases.author: joeraad
releases.description:
## Bug fixes

- **Empty menu when "Use native menus" is on (#3, #11):** Obsidian's native-menu mode stripped the toggles and buttons SnipDock attaches to the menu DOM. SnipDock now forces its menu to render as DOM regardless of the appearance setting.
- **Snippet toggles forgotten on quit (#10, #11):** Obsidian debounces its write to `appearance.json` by 1s with no flush-on-quit, so closing within that window dropped the change. SnipDock now flushes the config immediately after every enable/disable.
- **Master "paused" state was indistinguishable from "everything off" (#8):** when the master toggle is off but a remembered set exists, the row now reads *Paused · N remembered · tap to resume* in italic, with the full text on hover so it survives ellipsis truncation. The explicit "X" disable-all button also clears the remembered set.

## New feature

- **Column-first sorting for multi-column layout (#7):** new setting that fills each column top-to-bottom before moving to the next, instead of dealing snippets row-by-row. Off by default. Thanks @Bin-T.

## Cleanup

- `styles.css` now uses Obsidian theme variables throughout, so community themes restyle the menu seamlessly.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/joeraad/obsidian-snipdock/compare/1.1.2...1.1.3
releases.name: 1.1.3: native-menu fix, paused-state clarity, column-first sort

releases.author: joeraad
releases.description: Resyncs the plugin manifest with the GitHub release tag so the Obsidian community plugin store and direct installs both resolve to the same version. No functional changes since 1.1.1.
releases.name: Release 1.1.2: sync manifest with release tag

releases.author: joeraad
releases.description:
## v1.1.0

Three new (all optional, off by default) menu features:

### Anchor to status bar icon (#4)
New setting to open the menu directly above the SnipDock status-bar
icon instead of in the corner of the window. The menu stays pinned
just above the bar and grows/shrinks upward as the list changes.

###  Snippet search (#2)
New setting adds a search box to the top of the menu that filters
the snippet list as you type.

### Multi-column layout (#1)
New setting lays snippets out across multiple columns instead of a
single list. Configure the number of columns (2–6); snippets are
distributed evenly across them. When enabled, the "Menu width"
setting becomes "Column width". The menu is automatically kept
within the screen bounds at any column count.
releases.name: Release 1.1.0 Anchored menu, search, and multi-column layout

releases.author: joeraad
releases.description: Release artefacts (`main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`) are now signed with GitHub build-provenance attestations, so anyone can cryptographically verify each file was built from this repository at the tagged commit.
releases.name: Release 1.0.2: sign release artefacts with build provenance attestations

releases.author: joeraad
releases.description:
- Status-bar menu for toggling CSS snippets
- Master toggle that remembers state
- Create, rename, open snippets in your editor
- Configurable menu size and snippet template
releases.name: SnipDock release 1.0.0
